search_terms_used: strength training distance runners performance meta-analysis pubmed
resistance training running economy systematic review pubmed
concurrent training running strength interference pubmed
heavy resistance training endurance runners running economy pubmed
exercise injury prevention endurance runners meta-analysis pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: 7 meta-analyses/systematic reviews, 2 RCTs, 1 meta-analysis on concurrent interference. Topics: plyometrics vs heavy resistance (Eihara 2022), running economy in elite (Balsalobre-Fernandez 2016), performance effects by method (Llanos-Lagos 2024a), economy by speed (Llanos-Lagos 2024b), biomechanical mechanisms (Trowell 2020), physiological determinants (Blagrove 2018), plyometrics in highly trained (Saunders 2006), RE durability under fatigue (Zanini 2025), concurrent interference (Wilson 2012), injury prevention (Wu 2024). All running-specific populations.

papers.title: Effects of Strength Training on the Physiological Determinants of Middle- and Long-Distance Running Performance: A Systematic Review
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 24 studies on how strength training affects physiological determinants (RE, VO2max, anaerobic capacity, body composition, neuromuscular) of distance running performance.

papers.title: The Effect of Strength Training Methods on Middle-Distance and Long-Distance Runners' Athletic Performance: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed the effect of different strength training methods (high load, submaximal, plyometric, combined) on time trial performance, TTE, VO2max, vVO2max, sprint capacity, and MMSS in distance runners.

papers.title: Effect of Strength Training Programs in Middle- and Long-Distance Runners' Economy at Different Running Speeds: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed how strength training modalities differentially affect running economy at various speeds, finding plyometrics more effective at lower speeds and heavy loads more effective at higher speeds.

papers.title: Heavy Resistance Training Versus Plyometric Training for Improving Running Economy and Running Time Trial Performance: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Directly compared heavy resistance training vs plyometric training as supplements to running, finding heavy RT may be superior to plyometrics for improving running economy and time trial performance.

papers.title: Effects of Strength Training on Running Economy in Highly Trained Runners: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis of Controlled Trials
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed 5 controlled trials in elite-level runners (VO2max >60), finding strength training interventions of >4 weeks improved running economy by 2-8% without increasing body mass.

papers.title: Effect of Strength Training on Biomechanical and Neuromuscular Variables in Distance Runners: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed and meta-analyzed 25 studies on how concurrent strength and endurance training changes biomechanical and neuromuscular variables in distance runners.

papers.title: Short-term plyometric training improves running economy in highly trained middle and long distance runners
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: RCT examining 9 weeks of plyometric training (3x30 min/week) added to regular training in highly trained runners, demonstrating improved running economy without changes in VO2max.

papers.title: Strength Training Improves Running Economy Durability and Fatigued High-Intensity Performance in Well-Trained Male Runners: A Randomized Control Trial
papers.journal: Medicine and Science in Sports and Exercise
papers.notes: RCT showing 10 weeks of twice-weekly maximal strength and plyometric training improved running economy durability and high-intensity time to exhaustion at the end of a 90-minute run.

papers.title: Concurrent training: a meta-analysis examining interference of aerobic and resistance exercises
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Landmark meta-analysis of concurrent training interference, finding that running (but not cycling) combined with resistance training produced significant decrements in both hypertrophy and strength.

papers.title: Do Exercise-Based Prevention Programs Reduce Injury in Endurance Runners? A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed 9 studies on exercise-based injury prevention in endurance runners, finding no overall significant injury reduction but supervised interventions specifically showed significantly lower injury risk.
